tool.name: PICRUSt2 pick best domain
tool.command: pick_best_domain.py -n1 '$name_dom1' -n2 '$name_dom2' --tree_dom1 '$tree_dom1' --tree_dom2 '$tree_dom2' --tree_out_dom1 dom1_reduced_placed_seqs.tre --tree_out_dom2 dom2_reduced_placed_seqs.tre --nsti_table_dom1 '$nsti_table_dom1' --nsti_table_dom2 '$nsti_table_dom2' --nsti_table_out_dom1 dom1_reduced_marker_nsti_predicted.tsv --nsti_table_out_dom2 dom2_reduced_marker_nsti_predicted.tsv --nsti_table_out_combined combined_marker_nsti_predicted.tsv #if $ref_fasta_dom1 --ref_fasta_dom1 '$ref_fasta_dom1' #end if #if $ref_fasta_dom2 --ref_fasta_dom2 '$ref_fasta_dom2' #end if

param.label: Full tree for the 1st domain
param.help: containing both study sequences (i.e. ASVs or OTUs) and reference sequences

param.label: Full tree for the 2nd domain
param.help: containing both study sequences (i.e. ASVs or OTUs) and reference sequences

param.label: Reference FASTA file for the 1st domain
Reference FASTA file for the 2nd domain
Name of the domain associated with the 1st tree and NSTI table
Name of the domain associated with the 2nd tree and NSTI table

data.name: tree_out_dom1
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: reference tree for the 1st domain

data.name: tree_out_dom2
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: reference tree for the 2nd domain

data.name: nsti_table_out_dom1
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: NSTI table for the 1st domain

data.name: nsti_table_out_dom2
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: NSTI table for the 2nd domain

tool.help:
@HELP_HEADER@
pick best domain ================
This script takes in trees and NSTI tables for two domains (by default bacteria and archaea) and determines the best reference fit (lowest Nearest Sequences Taxon Index [NSTI]) for each of the study sequences. It then filters the trees and NSTI files for each domain so as they contain only the sequences that fit best within that domain.
Input =====
- The trees for the two domains (output of `place seqs`) - The NSTI tables for the two domains (output of `Hidden state prediction`)
Output ======
Filtered trees and tables.
